title: LG Unveils World’s Lightest 17-Inch RTX Laptop With Aerominum Build and AI at CES 2026
excerpt: LG has officially announced its 2026 LG gram laptop lineup ahead of the Consumer Electronics Show…
body:
LG has officially announced its 2026 LG gram laptop lineup ahead of the Consumer Electronics Show (CES 2026), spotlighting a major breakthrough in lightweight design and AI-enhanced computing. Headlining the range is the LG Gram Pro 17 (17Z90UR) — touted as the world’s lightest 17-inch laptop equipped with an NVIDIA RTX graphics card — made possible through a new aerospace-inspired material and advanced AI features.

The 2026 lineup also emphasizes durability, productivity, and cross-device connectivity, signaling LG’s strategic focus on versatility for creators, professionals, and power users.

Aerominum: Aerospace Quality Meets Laptop Portability

A key innovation for the 2026 LG gram series is Aerominum, a newly developed magnesium-aluminum alloy inspired by aerospace materials engineering. LG states this advanced composite delivers exceptional strength without adding weight, reinforcing the Gram’s structural durability while preserving its ultralight identity.

According to reports:

  • Aerominum improves scratch resistance and overall chassis robustness
  • The material meets military-grade durability standards
  • The result is a laptop that feels premium and sturdy without bulk — a perennial challenge for ultraportable systems.

This focus on material science allows LG to push the boundaries of size and performance, especially for larger-screen laptops that traditionally skew heavy.

LG Gram Pro 17: A New Benchmark for Large Laptops

Headlining the 2026 Gram lineup, the LG Gram Pro 17 brings together a 17-inch display and discrete graphics into a package comparable in size and weight to some 16-inch models — a rare feat in premium laptops.

Key specifications include:

  • 17-inch WQXGA (2560 × 1600) display for sharp visuals and productivity
  • NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5050 Laptop GPU with 8GB GDDR7 memory, targeting graphics-intensive tasks such as video editing, 3D design, and creative workflows
  • A chassis designed to balance portability and performance, ideal for professional creators who also value mobility.

LG claims this model will be available initially in North America, with pricing and wider availability expected to be announced around the CES event.

Dual AI Systems for Productivity and Creativity

True to current trends, the 2026 LG gram lineup introduces a dual AI strategy that blends on-device intelligence with cloud-based AI services. This approach aims to accelerate everyday tasks and creative workflows while maintaining flexibility and security.

Two core AI elements featured across select models are:

  • On-Device AI (EXAONE 3.5 sLLM): LG’s small local language model powers gram chat, enabling offline AI functions such as summarization, contextual search, and content creation even without internet connectivity.
  • Cloud-Assisted AI & Microsoft Copilot+ Support: For enhanced productivity, cloud-based AI tools provide advanced assistance, from real-time translation to advanced content generation. (Business Standard)

The combination promises a more responsive and personalized user experience that bridges lightweight hardware with AI-driven capabilities.

Cross-Device Connectivity With Gram Link

Recognizing that users today span multiple ecosystems, LG has upgraded gram Link functionality, expanding seamless interaction between the new Gram models and a wider array of devices.

The enhanced gram Link enables:

  • Wireless file transfer between Gram laptops and Android or iOS devices
  • Screen sharing and display extension with compatible devices
  • Integration with LG webOS products such as TVs and monitors
  • Unified continuity workflows across platforms without complex setup.

This expanded connectivity facilitates a more fluid experience for hybrid work, remote collaboration, and multimedia playback.

Expanded 2026 Lineup: Options for Every Type of User

The 2026 Gram family is broader and more versatile than in past years, offering machines tailored to different user profiles:

  • LG Gram Pro AI (17-inch & 16-inch): Ultralight workstations with discrete graphics and professional performance
  • LG Gram Pro 360 AI (16-inch): Convertible model for flexible modes of usage
  • LG Gram AI (15-inch & 14-inch): Smaller form factors with hybrid AI productivity
  • LG Gram Book AI: Traditional clamshell focused on balanced portability and everyday productivity.

The expanded range reflects LG’s attempt to “meet users where they work,” offering both ultraportability and creative performance across screen sizes and use cases.

Real-World Use Cases: Productivity, Content Creation, and Beyond

The introduction of a powerful 17-inch RTX laptop in such a lightweight chassis has clear implications for creators and professionals:

  • Creative Professionals: Video editors, graphic designers, and 3D artists benefit from discrete GPU acceleration without sacrificing mobility. (Business Standard)
  • Hybrid Workers: The dual AI and connectivity features accelerate content management, search, and productivity tasks across devices.
  • Students and Productivity Users: On-device AI tools streamline notes, summaries, and research workflows without constant internet reliance.

Rather than pushing a one-size-fits-all narrative, LG’s strategy suggests a nuanced understanding of how performance, weight, and intelligent features impact different workflows.

Looking Ahead: What LG’s Strategy Reveals About Laptop Trends

LG’s 2026 Gram lineup exemplifies several major trends shaping the PC market:

  • Extreme Portability Without Compromise: Through materials like Aerominum, devices can remain light while supporting high-end components.
  • Hybrid AI Integration: Blending on-device intelligence with cloud services is becoming a standard for productivity platforms. (Business Standard)
  • Cross-Platform Fluidity: As users engage with smartphones, tablets, TVs, and PCs, unified connectivity ecosystems become more valuable.

LG’s innovations reflect broader industry momentum toward smarter, lighter, and more connected laptops designed for modern creative and professional workflows.

Conclusion: Redefining What a “Portable Powerhouse” Can Be

The 2026 LG Gram lineup demonstrates that ultralight laptops no longer have to compromise performance or features. By leveraging Aerominum material, advanced hybrid AI, and enhanced connectivity, LG has crafted machines that blur the lines between ultraportable notebooks and powerful workstations.

The LG Gram Pro 17 stands out as a symbol of this evolution — delivering RTX-class graphics in the lightest 17-inch form factor yet, while AI-driven features address real-world productivity needs. With this launch, LG is setting a new benchmark for what “portable performance” means in 2026.
